threatgraph_indicators.description: A process has opened a handle to a Falcon process with excessive permissions requested. If this is unexpected, review the process tree and surrounding events.

A process has written a suspicious file to disk. Adversaries may write a malicious file to a commonly trusted directory, use a benign name, or a mismatched file extension. This is done for the sake of evading defenses and observation. Check the activity and surrounding events are expected in your environment.
A process associated with a known ransomware campaign launched. Investigate the host for signs of a ransomware attack.
This process created and injected code into another process, a common Process Migration technique. Review the process tree.

Rundll32 launched a suspended process. This might be malware hijacking system processes and launching suspended processes as hollowing targets. Investigate the process tree and the source of the injection.

description: A process associated with a known ransomware campaign launched. Investigate the host for signs of a ransomware attack.
name: ProcRansomware

cmdline: %SystemRoot%\system32\csrss.exe ObjectDirectory=\Windows SharedSection=1024,20480,768 Windows=On SubSystemType=Windows ServerDll=basesrv,1 ServerDll=winsrv:UserServerDllInitialization,3 ServerDll=sxssrv,4 ProfileControl=Off MaxRequestThreads=16
description: A process performed reads of user key buffers. Adversaries often use this to intercept passwords and other sensitive information. Review the process tree.
name: PollingKeyloggingInformational

threatgraph_indicators.description: A process launched that shares characteristics with mimikatz, a password dumping utility. mimikatz's primary purpose is to steal passwords. If credentials were dumped, change your passwords and investigate the process tree.
A process appears to have performed a system call by bypassing UMPPC hooks.
A process appears to be suspiciously executing code via Powershell. Investigate the command line and process tree.
An executable with two file name extensions was written. Adversaries can masquerade malware with a seemingly safe file extension to evade detection. Review the executable.
A process appears to be suspiciously executing code via Powershell. Investigate the command line and process tree.
Command prompt in process subtree beneath a productivity application
A suspicious process installed itself along with other bundled software. This process is likely malicious and might be a variant of DealPly. Review the apps installed.
A process associated with a known ransomware campaign launched. Investigate the host for signs of a ransomware attack.
A process loaded a module that shares characteristics with a known malicious file. Review the modules loaded by the process.
A process performed reads of user key buffers. Adversaries often use this to intercept passwords and other sensitive information. Review the process tree.
A process appears to have performed a system call by bypassing UMPPC hooks.
